description: UiAccessHandler is a command-layer handler (delegated to from the @Route methods in TreasuryAPICommand for /treasuryapi ui ...) yet it injects ExplorerUiMapper directly and performs all persistence (role add/remove/list, link-code lookup/redemption, identity upsert) straight from the handler. There is no ExplorerUiService interface+impl. This violates the stated layering rule that services are the ONLY layer calling mappers and that a command handler must not inject a mapper. It also means input validation (VALID_ROLES check, role normalisation) and the link/keycloak orchestration live in the command layer rather than a re-validating service. The sibling key handlers (PersonalKeyHandler, BusinessKeyHandler) correctly route through ApiKeyService, making this an inconsistency as well as a violation.
recommendation: Introduce an ExplorerUiService (interface + impl) that owns ExplorerUiMapper and KeycloakAdminClient, performs role validation and the link/keycloak orchestration (throwing semantic exceptions on invalid role / unknown link code), and expose only that service to UiAccessHandler. Move mapper injection out of the handler.
verification_note: Snippet verified verbatim at the cited lines. UiAccessHandler (in commands/) is @Injected with ExplorerUiMapper (line 32/39-41) and calls it directly: addRole (86), removeRole (96), listRoles (106), findValidLinkSub (113), upsertIdentity (119), deleteLinkCode (120) — all raw persistence. ExplorerUiMapper is a genuine MyBatis @Mapper with INSERT/DELETE/SELECT. No ExplorerUiService exists in services/ (only ApiKeyService and KeycloakAdminClient). Input validation (VALID_ROLES, role .toLowerCase normalisation) and the link/Keycloak orchestration (lookup→upsert→delete→setMinecraftAttributes) all live in the handler. Sibling handler PersonalKeyHandler correctly injects ApiKeyService (never a mapper), confirming the inconsistency. This directly violates the CLAUDE.md rule 'A command must not inject a mapper' and 'persistence holds all DB access / command handler must not inject a mapper', which CLAUDE.md explicitly labels a defect. Severity major is fair — a genuine, in-module-inconsistent layering violation, not a correctness/money blocker.

description: UiAccessHandler injects the Guice Injector and calls injector.getInstance(GroupReconciliationTask.class) as a service locator from business/command code, which the DI-discipline charter explicitly flags. The lazy-resolve is used to avoid a hard binding when LuckPerms is absent, but this is exactly a service-locator antipattern; a Provider<GroupReconciliationTask> (or a guarded optional binding surfaced behind a small service) would express the optionality without handing the whole injector to a handler.
recommendation: Replace the injected Injector + injector.getInstance(...) with an injected com.google.inject.Provider<GroupReconciliationTask> (or move self-sync behind a service that owns the optional dependency). Remove the Injector field from the handler.

snippet:
String display = roles.isEmpty() ? "player (no elevated roles)" : String.join(", ", roles);
        message.send(sender, "treasuryapi.ui.user.list", "player", playerName, "roles", display);
description: The empty-roles fallback text 'player (no elevated roles)' is a hardcoded English player-facing string interpolated into the {roles} placeholder of the treasuryapi.ui.user.list message, rather than being sourced from a Message key. The charter requires all player-facing text via Message keys. (String.join of role names is data, not a concern; the hardcoded literal is.)
recommendation: Add a Message key (e.g. treasuryapi.ui.user.no-roles) and either send a distinct message when roles is empty or resolve the fallback via Message, so the string is localisable and consistent with the rest of the messaging.

snippet:
String status = key.isRevoked() ? "Revoked" : "Active";
            String expiry = key.isRevoked() ? "—" : EXP_FMT.format(key.getExpiresAt());
description: The list-entry status labels 'Revoked'/'Active' and the '—' expiry placeholder are hardcoded English player-facing strings built in the handler and passed into the {status}/{expiry} message placeholders, rather than being Message keys. The same pattern is duplicated verbatim in BusinessKeyHandler (doList and doListAccess). Player-facing text should come from Message keys per the charter.
recommendation: Introduce Message keys for the status labels and the empty-expiry marker (e.g. treasuryapi.status.active / .revoked) and resolve them via Message instead of inline literals, in all three list loops.

description: Authorisation for reissue/revoke is enforced entirely in the command-layer handlers (canManage / getOwnerUuid checks), not in the service. ApiKeyService.reissueKey/revokeKey take only a keyId and perform no ownership/proprietorship re-validation, so the service trusts the handler to have authorised. The charter requires services to re-validate inputs and enforce authorisation (don't trust handlers), throwing semantic exceptions. A second caller of ApiKeyService could rotate or revoke any key by id with no ownership check. Same gap in PersonalKeyHandler.doReissue/doRevoke.
recommendation: Push the ownership/proprietorship authorisation into ApiKeyService (pass the acting UUID, throw NoPermissionException/NotFoundException from the service), so the invariant holds regardless of caller; keep the handler's pre-check only for user-friendly messaging.
